formulas.name: The Road Not Taken
formulas.formula: The poet's choice of the road will affect his life forever.
formulas.notes: This poem discusses the idea that the choices we make in life can lead to different outcomes and opportunities.

formulas.name: The World Is Too Much With Us
formulas.formula: Nature has been neglected by humans, leading to its degradation.
formulas.notes: This poem highlights the importance of appreciating and preserving nature.

formulas.name: Ozymandias
formulas.formula: Arrogance and pride can lead to downfall.
formulas.notes: This poem cautions against the dangers of arrogance and the fleeting nature of power.

formulas.name: The Address
formulas.formula: The speaker's tone and emotions convey the complexity of relationships.
formulas.notes: This poem explores the intricacies of human relationships and the emotions that come with them.

formulas.name: The Lady of Shalott
formulas.formula: The speaker's actions are driven by her emotions and experiences.
formulas.notes: This poem explores the idea of the speaker's emotional state and how it affects her actions.

formulas.name: The Eve of St. Agnes
formulas.formula: The speaker's emotions and surroundings contribute to the atmosphere.
formulas.notes: This poem highlights the importance of setting and atmosphere in creating a mood.

formulas.name: La Belle Dame sans Merci
formulas.formula: The speaker's perception of reality is distorted by his emotions.
formulas.notes: This poem explores the idea of how emotions can alter one's perception of reality.

formulas.name: No Coward Soul Is Mine
formulas.formula: The speaker's faith and confidence guide her through life's challenges.
formulas.notes: This poem emphasizes the importance of faith and confidence in overcoming obstacles.
